transcript.clean_text: lavuelter resuming hostilities after the final rest day playing with the northern coast of spain and a spectacular run 180 kilometers to santa cruz how would it pan out nobody knew and the reason was despite the fact everyone was saying it was a sprinter's day there was plenty of climbs along the way in fact over two thousand two hundred meters of altitude gain and a technical finale while taking it on were five brave souls devolve yetzerbal bizkara dimitri glace and quinn simmons all thought they had a chance so did everybody else until a big crash within the peloton took a few riders out including chicony and rudy monard others carried their bruises and indeed scars throughout the remainder of the day john boo particularly badly cut up well the breakaway was not allowed to have too much of a leash it was down to a minute and a half when lotta siddal thought they would join the fun harm van hulk trying to bridge over meanwhile mountains points and sprint points were going the way of yetzerbal for burgos bh so the five became six but the gap what would happen to that a minute and 23 that was before ua team emirates decided to put the hammer down on the rises and falls of this terrain they were making life difficult for all of the sprinters not least their particular target fabio jacobson in the green no panic for de kernig quickstep they had two men in the front group indeed the rest of the team were back to help fabio jackson get back in well get back in he did but the pace change had killed off the break essentially 30 seconds the gap had narrowed to by the time we were at the 40 kilometer mark it did stretch just a little bit but other sprinters teams started to get interested in the finale francis de gea of course working for arnold de mar wanted to engage and help out likewise dsm working for dainese stand of author's last man standing from the breakaway finally tagged with four and a half kilometers to go and the last climb of many on the day well a highly technical entrance into town indeed twists and turns along the way dropped chains just booking a few as you can see into kernig were doing their best to make sure that at least there was a chance for jakobson in the end he would have to do it on his own joe cerney the last man for de koenig out front coming around him doing a superb lead out job but essentially for the wrong man alex krieger of germany thought he had sasha modelo on his wheel anything but it was yuri mouse who was there and in fact when he launched jacobson took his wheel getting a good screen and spearing through the center trenton also getting involved likewise matthews but fabio jacobson it was that took the day to make it three stage wins for him on this welter securing his position in that green jersey as we head to the high ground and full of thanks not only for his team but for those medical staff that had helped him to these three wins so far it was a brutal day that ended maybe as predicted jakobson taking it in green living up to the building roaring across the line like the incredible hulk all dressed in green and ready to party great time for him and indeed for his the kernick team yakuza has been taking it ahead of maos trenton matthews and azee rounding out the top five abroad story oliveira minali soto and venturini in the top ten staying in the red jersey odd christian iking as we head to the high ground of the mountains with two special climbs back to back over the next two days viking leads overall guillaume martin by 54 seconds and pretty much roglic by a minute and 36 mass lopez hague bernal yates all in the frame points classification well of course the green jersey belongs to fabio jacobson and he leads by almost twice as many points as material 20. 250 for jakobson 123 for trenton the nearest man to him with the sprinter's days now behind us so what lies ahead kovadonga is the first of two days of special climbing and what a special day it will be big gaps surely by the end of the day a category top one test taken twice as part of a loop and then that special climb at the very end of the day the northern coast of spain is brutal we know that even on a sprinter's day how will it be when we hit the high ground
